title: MELANOTAN 1
description: Melanotan 1 (Afamelanotide) is a synthetic analog of alpha-MSH that stimulates melanin production, causing skin darkening without sun exposure. It's FDA-approved for certain conditions and provides photoprotection for those with light sensitivity.

research.title: In vitro evaluation of Poly(d,l-lactide-co-glycolide) polymer-based implants containing the α-melanocyte stimulating hormone analog, Melanotan-I
research.summary: Study examined release patterns of Melanotan-I from biodegradable PLGA implants, observing three-phase release.

research.title: An unhealthy glow? A review of melanotan use and associated clinical outcomes
research.summary: Literature review on Melanotan I and II effects, identifying minor and serious adverse effects including toxidrome and melanoma.

research.title: Controlled-Release Delivery System for the α-MSH Analog Melanotan-I Using Poloxamer 407
research.summary: Researchers developed controlled-release formulations using poloxamer 407 with additives for sustained delivery.

research.title: In vitro characterization and in vivo release profile of a poly (d,l-lactide-co-glycolide)-based implant delivery system for the α-MSH analog, Melanotan-I
research.summary: Researchers developed biodegradable copolymer implants for sustained Melanotan-I delivery, demonstrating steady release over one month.

research.title: Effect of MELANOTAN®, [Nle4, D-Phe7]-α-MSH, on melanin synthesis in humans with MC1R variant alleles
research.summary: Study found MELANOTAN® significantly increased melanin, especially in those with MC1R genetic variants.

sentiment.summary: Users seeking tanning without sun exposure report good results. Slower acting than MT-2 but considered safer. FDA approval for EPP lends legitimacy.

effects.label: Slower Than MT-2:
effects.description: Takes longer to produce visible tanning.

dosage.label: Loading Phase:
dosage.description: Often 1mg daily until desired tan achieved.
